subject: Anthropology
title: Kinship Charts and Diagrams
description: A short lesson to help you apply Kinship Charts and Diagrams.

hook.text: Triangles, circles, and lines. Anthropologists map families like engineers map circuits. Every symbol tells a story.

buildup.text: Kinship diagrams use standard symbols to represent relationships: triangles for men, circles for women, lines for ties.

discovery.text: These charts reveal patterns invisible in conversation: who marries whom, which lines inherit, and how clans connect.

twist.text: Diagrams simplify but also distort. They flatten messy, fluid relationships into neat, static categories.

climax.text: Kinship charts are powerful tools but imperfect mirrors. Real families are always messier than any diagram.

punchline.text: A map of the family is not the family itself.

quiz.question: What do kinship diagrams reveal?
options: Patterns of marriage, inheritance, and clan connection
